summary: Crisp soundboard recording captures a high‑energy 9/22/87 show highlighted by a stellar Two Soldiers Jam and fire‑filled China > Rider > Samson > Terrapin sequence.
review: The September‑22‑1987 set is praised for its enthusiasm, tight first set and an incendiary second set that features the famed Two Soldiers Jam, Handsome Cabin Boy motif and a soaring China > Rider > Samson > Terrapin stretch.  The audience‑sourced board (sbd) tape is described as crisp and clear, with only minor mix quirks such as a low‑volume Bob Weir guitar on “China Cat.” Overall the performance is considered one of the stronger shows of that Philly run.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating is ~4.2/5, with most reviewers highlighting strong musical moments and a clean soundboard source; only occasional minor performance or mix issues were noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear board tape praised by many reviewers; no major drop‑outs. Some comments about a low Bob Weir guitar level on “China Cat” but overall clean mix.

poor_songs: Rider (noted as below average by one reviewer)
China Cat (Bob Weir guitar low in mix)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as ripping with solid energy; second set builds to a fire‑filled jam stretch (China‑Rider‑Samson‑Terrapin) and strong closers. Minor sloppiness noted but overall tight and fun.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for adventurous plucking, dramatic full‑tone excursions and overall great playing; a few reviewers note thin vocals on “Baby Blue.”
band_member_comments.Bob: Generally positive (“hardly any lyrical errors”), but one reviewer cites his guitar being too low in the “China Cat” segment.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s Hammond organ highlighted as bright, well‑timed and a key part of the sound; occasional “modicum” comment but overall favorable.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums/Space section praised for its extended tease (Playin') and transition into The Wheel; regarded as solid rhythmic foundation.
